description: E2E test case designs for opencode-auth crate - Authentication and credentials

test_cases.title: API key storage and retrieval
steps: Store API key with provider name

test_cases.title: JWT algorithm confusion attack
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: JWT must reject 'none' algorithm or algorithm mismatch
test_cases.risk_addressed: Attacker forges tokens by changing algorithm header
test_cases.notes: JWT validation uses jsonwebtoken with HS256 algorithm validation

test_cases.title: JWT claims validation
test_cases.description: JWT must validate exp, nbf, iat, iss claims strictly
steps: Test expired token rejection via validation
test_cases.notes: Claims exp validation handled by jsonwebtoken library

test_cases.title: JWT secret/key rotation
test_cases.description: Tokens signed with old key must be rejected after rotation
test_cases.risk_addressed: Tokens signed with compromised old key still valid
steps: Attempt to validate token signed with V1
expected_outcomes: Token signed with old key rejected
New tokens signed with V2 accepted
Key ID (kid) claim used for correct key lookup
test_cases.notes: Requires code change for key rotation support

test_cases.title: API key not logged or exposed
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Stored API keys must never appear in logs
steps: Verify raw file does not contain plaintext keys
expected_outcomes: Key redacted in any error messages
Key only used for actual API calls

test_cases.title: API key storage encryption
test_cases.description: API keys at rest must be encrypted, not plaintext
test_cases.risk_addressed: API keys readable from storage file
steps: Verify encrypted storage file does not contain plaintext
expected_outcomes: Cannot read key from storage file
Encryption key derived from master password or keychain

test_cases.title: API key deletion is permanent
test_cases.description: Deleted API keys must not be recoverable

test_cases.title: OAuth state parameter validation
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: OAuth flow must validate state to prevent CSRF
steps: Verify callback with correct state accepted

test_cases.title: OAuth token storage and refresh
test_cases.description: OAuth tokens stored securely and refreshed automatically
test_cases.risk_addressed: OAuth tokens expired and not refreshed, causing auth failures
test_cases.preconditions: OAuth tokens stored with refresh token

test_cases.title: OAuth refresh token rotation
test_cases.description: Refresh token rotation prevents replay of old tokens
test_cases.risk_addressed: Stolen refresh token can be reused
steps: Use refresh token to get new tokens
Attempt to use old refresh token
expected_outcomes: New refresh token required for next refresh

test_cases.title: Session token binding to device/fingerprint
test_cases.description: Session tokens should be bindable to device ID
test_cases.risk_addressed: Stolen session token used on different device
steps: Use session on same device - verify works
Use session on different device - verify rejected or needs re-auth
expected_outcomes: Different device session rejected or flagged

critical_invariants: API keys never logged or stored plaintext
high_risk_scenarios: auth_cred_001: API key leaked in logs
